name: Indiana Democratic Party
subject: Fast Facts Friday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party
body:
| | | |
---|---
| | **-May 22, 2020-** **-Indiana's first vote-by-mail election-** **** Yesterday was the deadline to apply to vote by mail in Indiana's first-ever opportunity to let all registered voters vote by mail, regardless of reason. We continue to believe it is the safest thing for voters and poll workers. Hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers applied to vote by mail. Hoosier Dems stepped up to protect each other and make their voices heard. This is more than just a response to the pandemic. It is a step toward making Indiana's elections more convenient and inclusive. Thank you for making history and proving that voting by mail works. If you were one of the thousands of Democrats that applied to vote by mail, we want to hear from you! Tell us about your experience **_[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594375/199562011/1115516009?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535)_**. **-INDems' virtual Hoosier Hospitality Dinner- ** **** Join us for our virtual [Hoosier Hospitality Dinner](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594376/199562012/-1853952297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) on June 12! This is your chance to hear from Democrats across the country and be a part of the Party's work in 2020. You won't want to miss it! Watch for the announcement of our keynote speaker on Tuesday, May 26. Visit our [2020 ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594377/199562013/-1853952297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535)[HH Dinner page](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594378/199562014/-1853952297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) for more information. **-Holcomb's mega-donor gets multi-million dollar state contract-** **** In March, Grant Verstandig, the Chief Digital Officer at Optum Health, dumped [$50,000](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594379/199562015/1817612310?results=true&LastName=&LastNameSearchType=1&Address=&City=&State=&Zip=&FinanceCategoryID=-32768&ContributionCodeID=1&ContributionAmountMinimum=20000&ContributionAmountMaximum=-32768&ContributionDateBegin=1%2f1%2f2019&ContributionDateEnd=12%3a00%3a00+AM&MajorContribution=0&CommitteeCandidateDisplayMode=0&CommitteeName=&CommitteeID=-32768&CommitteeOrgCodeID=-32768&CommitteeNameSearchType=&CandidateOffice=3&CandidateDistrictNumber=&CandidateParty=-32768&Exploratory=&CandidateFirstName=&CandidateLastName=Hol&CandidateLastNameSearchType=1&CandidateFirstNameSearchType=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) into Holcomb's re-election campaign. Then, late last month Optum Health received a $17.9 million contract from the state to conduct COVID-19 testing. They were the [only testing company](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594380/199562016/187506509?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) that the Holcomb administration asked for a quote. Yep. Holcomb's deep-pocketed political donors were fast-tracked to a multi-million dollar state contract. **-Todd Rokita: the GOP's 11th-hour Attorney General candidate-** **** This week, former congressman Todd Rokita entered the Republican race for Attorney General. His eleventh-hour entrance comes after the Indiana Supreme Court [suspended Curtis Hill's law license](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594381/199562017/1125639498?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) for 30 days due to allegations of sexual misconduct from four women. Rokita, who infamously [lost the GOP's primary](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594382/199562018/-1634157721?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) for the U.S. Senate to Mike Braun in 2018, is gasoline on the housefire that is the Republican's forlorn race to keep the AG's seat. His candidacy is a clear indicator of the GOP's lack of confidence in Curtis Hill. But with Hill refusing to drop out, Rokita will all but ensure that the GOP Attorney General's race will go from an embarrassing effort to a bare-knuckled brawl. **-Nursing homes aren't receiving the care they need-** **** In New York, where nursing home residents account for about [25 percent](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594383/199562019/-1950629872?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) of deaths, the governor is mandating [twice-weekly](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594384/199562020/1515268750?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) testing of staff. In Indiana, where nursing home deaths make up a staggering [41 percent](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594385/199562021/1634335991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) of total COVID19-related fatalities, the Holcomb administration has only offered excuses. Even as Holcomb continues to lift safety regulations, the state has failed to provide[ widespread testing](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594386/199562022/746575489?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) to nursing homes. The [White House recommends](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594387/199562023/-1015424583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535) that all residents and staff members of nursing homes be tested. Unlike other states, the Holcomb administration has not required nursing homes to report which facilities are [experiencing outbreaks](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17594388/199562024/786242265?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjM4YzlkNi02NjljLWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SdxGwESXwbsmc9mBcvRl2ZzyQ7DKxHaXyda245w2ciY=&emci=9e48ed38-2d9c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=5f38c9d6-669c-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=4925535), leaving many Hoosiers to wonder about the safety of their loved ones. The Holcomb administration's inadequate response not only puts our loved ones' lives at risk but also leaves us in the dark. Hoosiers deserve better. **-Resources-** **_Did you apply to vote by mail? clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | -May 22, 2020- -Indiana's first vote-by-mail election- ** Yesterday was the deadline to apply to vote by mail in Indiana's first-ever opportunity to let all registered voters vote by mail, regardless of reason. We continue to believe it is the safest thing for voters and poll workers. Hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers applied to vote by mail. Hoosier Dems stepped up to protect each other and make their voices heard. This is more than just a response to the pandemic. It is a step toward making Indiana's elections more convenient and inclusive. Thank you for making history and proving that voting by mail works. If you were one of the thousands of Democrats that applied to vote by mail, we want to hear from you! Tell us about your experience . -INDems' virtual Hoosier Hospitality Dinner- Join us for our virtual on June 12! This is your chance to hear from Democrats across the country and be a part of the Party's work in 2020. You won't want to miss it! Watch for the announcement of our keynote speaker on Tuesday, May 26. Visit our for more information. -Holcomb's mega-donor gets multi-million dollar state contract- In March, Grant Verstandig, the Chief Digital Officer at Optum Health, dumped into Holcomb's re-election campaign. Then, late last month Optum Health received a $17.9 million contract from the state to conduct COVID-19 testing. They were the that the Holcomb administration asked for a quote. Yep. Holcomb's deep-pocketed political donors were fast-tracked to a multi-million dollar state contract. -Todd Rokita: the GOP's 11th-hour Attorney General candidate- This week, former congressman Todd Rokita entered the Republican race for Attorney General. His eleventh-hour entrance comes after the Indiana Supreme Court for 30 days due to allegations of sexual misconduct from four women. Rokita, who infamously for the U.S. Senate to Mike Braun in 2018, is gasoline on the housefire that is the Republican's forlorn race to keep the AG's seat. His candidacy is a clear indicator of the GOP's lack of confidence in Curtis Hill. But with Hill refusing to drop out, Rokita will all but ensure that the GOP Attorney General's race will go from an embarrassing effort to a bare-knuckled brawl. -Nursing homes aren't receiving the care they need- In New York, where nursing home residents account for about of deaths, the governor is mandating testing of staff. In Indiana, where nursing home deaths make up a staggering of total COVID19-related fatalities, the Holcomb administration has only offered excuses. Unlike other states, the Holcomb administration has not required nursing homes to report which facilities are , leaving many Hoosiers to wonder about the safety of their loved ones. The Holcomb administration's inadequate response not only puts our loved ones' lives at risk but also leaves us in the dark. Hoosiers deserve better. -Resources- Did you apply to vote by mail? name: Mike Thompson
subject: [Sign On] Pass the Heroes Act!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/j5bu0OivMUo2e4o2T8gVoUr08IIHYZNlmmSP7e-FUuM=/3P6FveI5QyPTEnJFUA0ltHP2vDtAjmQZb6YobsKLrmM=)

Peter,

Last week the House passed the much needed Heroes Act. The $3 trillion dollar coronavirus relief package would include another round of stimulus checks, expand coronavirus testing, add $10 billion to the SNAP program, add additional rental and mortgage support, and more.

We need to pass the Heroes Act **_Immediately_** , but Mitch McConnell is stalling. [**_Will you join us and demand Mitch McConnell call a vote on the Heroes Act in the Senate?_**](https://act.myngp.com/el/j5bu0OivMUo2e4o2T8gVoUr08IIHYZNlmmSP7e-FUuM=/3P6FveI5QyPTEnJFUA0ltHP2vDtAjmQZb6YobsKLrmM=)

**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.myngp.com/el/j5bu0OivMUo2e4o2T8gVoUr08IIHYZNlmmSP7e-FUuM=/3P6FveI5QyPTEnJFUA0ltHP2vDtAjmQZb6YobsKLrmM=)**
The Heroes Act will offer relief to Americans who have lost their jobs, who can no longer afford to pay their rent or mortgages, who work as an essential employee, and others who are struggling in a myriad of ways. It would also include support for state and local officials and will protect our essential workers, such as firefighters and police officers.

[**_Add your name now to show Mitch McConnell that we need the Heroes Act immediately._**](https://act.myngp.com/el/j5bu0OivMUo2e4o2T8gVoUr08IIHYZNlmmSP7e-FUuM=/3P6FveI5QyPTEnJFUA0ltHP2vDtAjmQZb6YobsKLrmM=)

I will continue to advocate for those who have been affected by the coronavirus. Thank you for your continued support.

Thank you,

Mike Thompson

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: A picture of progress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
This is a picture of progress:

More than 1,000 people signed up for our voter registration phone bank on
Monday.

We all Zoomed into a video gathering at 6:30pm, got ourselves pumped up,
and were off to the races, our first phone calls going out ten minutes
later.

By 8:30, this group of amazing, persistent volunteers had made more than
80,000 phone calls to known Democrats who’d recently moved to Texas.

We called people who had reliably voted in the Democratic primary in their
former home state, but have yet to update their voter registration in
Texas. They’d been mailed voter registration forms a few days before our
calls. We were calling to remind them that all they have to do now is sign
it and put it in the mail.

Some people made 20 phone calls, some made over 100. I was able to make 88
over the two hours of our phone bank. Some wrong numbers here and there
(which is OK, helps us to clean up our list) along with more than a few
answering machines... but when you’d connect with someone it sure felt
good.

My favorite was a special education teacher who lives outside of San
Antonio. She answered the phone as she was walking her dog. “I’m so glad
you called me!” she said. “I’ve got that form on top of the rest of all my
paperwork and bills. I’ll sign it and send it in tomorrow!”

We talked a little more and she told me how hard it is to teach in the
midst of this pandemic. I shared my newfound respect for her after helping
Amy supervise our kids’ distance learning with their teachers here in El
Paso and told her about the wonderful special ed teachers my sister Erin’s
had along the way. I wanted to her to know that we really appreciate what
she’s doing.

At the end of our phone bank, Kate told us how many calls we’d made (big
cheers at the news that we cleared 80,000) and then drew two names for our
big prize: home delivery of O’Rourke-baked chocolate chip cookies (our
winners were Bella from Flushing, NY and Letti from Laredo, TX).

This coming Tuesday, we’ll do it again — we have over 1,400 people already
signed up to make calls — and then otra vez on Thursday.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42506?source=ema20200522-90912&t=1001&akid=90912%2E444211%2ElDRDEn ]Would you be up to join me Thursday, May 28th to make calls to
unregistered voters in Texas? If you say yes, you might very well make the
difference in helping us win the State House and stopping Donald Trump.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42506?source=ema20200522-90912&t=1002&akid=90912%2E444211%2ElDRDEn ]Sign up

We’ll keep doing this until we’ve called nearly one million of our fellow
Texans to make sure they get registered.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42546?refcode=ema20200522-90912&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=90912%2E444211%2ElDRDEn ](Your donations and support make this possible! If you want to keep
this going and make sure we register all these known Democrats in Texas,
please chip in!)

We are making the most of everyone’s time at home by helping to create the
conditions that will allow us to elect a Democratic majority in the Texas
State House and stop Donald Trump by denying him our state's 38 electoral
college votes.

Very grateful to be doing this work and to have your support to get it
done!

Thank you,

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=90912%2E444211%2ElDRDEn

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA

subject: Your tax dollars could go to illegals if Pelosi has her way
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathaleen Wall for Congress
body:
Pelosi's stimulus package is garbage | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/53fc13548451/release-kathaleen-wall-responds-to-the-house-vote-to-impeach-president-trump-12458561?e=01c99b7fbd)
---
| |
---
| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE:** Friday, May 22, 2020
---
| | **Conservative Kathaleen Wall Again Takes Issue With Nancy Pelosi's Big Spending Plans That Include Blue State Bailouts And Payments To Illegal Aliens: "It's A Non-Starter" **

_Sugar Land Republican Says: “Nancy Pelosi Will Never Stop With Her Spending Schemes, And This Is Why We Have To Win Back The House. Sri Kulkarni Will Simply Be Nancy Pelosi’s Lap Dog, And We Can’t Afford Either One Of Them.”_
**(Sugar Land, TX)** – Sugar Land Republican Congressional Candidate (TX-22) Kathaleen Wall today again took issue with Nancy Pelosi’s $3 trillion so-called “Heroes Act,” this time calling any notion of blue state bailouts “a non-starter.”

“Governor Abbott isn’t working around the clock to get Texas’ economy safely reopened only to have all of our taxpayer dollars shipped off to bailout blue states who are fiscally irresponsible or to those who are here illegally,” said Wall. “Nancy Pelosi will never stop with her spending schemes, and this is why we have to win back the House. Sri Kulkarni will simply be Nancy Pelosi’s lap dog, and we can’t afford either one of them.”

In addition to hammering Speaker Pelosi for the idea of shipping $875 billion in state bailouts and offering payments to illegal aliens, Wall has continually highlighted the more egregious examples of Pelosi’s spending package that saw 14 House Democrats defect, including:

* $375 billion in local government bailouts.
* $25 billion for the U.S. Postal Service.
* $10 million for the National Endowment for the Arts.
* $5 million for new Capitol Hill laptops and cell phones.
* A SALT tax cut for the [wealthy](https://KathaleenWall.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6732b2a961d1a34b05e3194bb&id=bf68997841&e=01c99b7fbd).

Kulkarni has been a vocal supporter of Nancy Pelosi throughout the 2020 campaign and has refused to criticize the Speaker on anything. Instead, Wall has argued, Congress’ focus should be on how to help reopen our country rapidly, safely and smartly.

“We’re going to fight to reopen Texas’ economy and then I’m going to make sure Nancy Pelosi doesn’t give all of our money away to blue states with management problems,” added Wall. “And never, under any circumstance, should we give away American dollars to people living in our country illegally.”

###
---
|
---

---

Box 1429Sugar Land, TX 77487 You can or .

**First** , China failed to come clean on the severeness of COVID-19.
**Then** , they lied about their country's total number of cases and refused to allow the WHO to investigate the virus' origins.

**Now** , the Chinese Communist Party is attempting to further cover up its role in the COVID-19 pandemic through online propaganda and disinformation.

Cory Gardner here, just reaching out to make sure that you didn't miss our new ad launch last Friday. In case you haven't gotten a chance to see it yet, check out the clip ASAP.
_Our 2014 victory here in Colorado was a watershed moment on behalf of Republicans nationwide. Our win proved that similar to the rest of the country, Coloradans were fed up with the Democrats ' empty promises. And in the last six years, I've tirelessly fought to uplift my fellow Americans through pro-growth policies and common-sense legislating.

And this year, I'm facing another critical Battleground Election. National Democrats are investing millions in Colorado to flip the Senate from red to blue._

name: Adem Bunkeddeko
subject: We won the debate!
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/0SE2FnahfUAcJW-KeAy5qpG6aKDxZOWrPnbjzvpAhj0=/E8qx-aYn-nnchTeM8pYYQs5HkiqZPWo-XhL5AolU0so=)

Peter,

In this new age of virtual campaigning, all aspects of our race have gone fully digital -- even the debate stage. So when News 12 Brooklyn hosted their first Zoom debate featuring myself and the other candidates, I used it as an opportunity to highlight the failed leadership that has led to this moment, and to share my vision for a way forward.

**[No other candidate can match our progressive platform or our grassroots momentum, so will you consider making a weekly recurring contribution on the heels of our winning debate performance?](https://act.myngp.com/el/0SE2FnahfUAcJW-KeAy5qpG6aKDxZOWrPnbjzvpAhj0=/PSB_2LlEFykglQ8vrmIrj5mE4B_ujeC_GRgVTUr2tK0=)**

******[MAKE A WEEKLY CONTRIBUTION](https://act.myngp.com/el/0SE2FnahfUAcJW-KeAy5qpG6aKDxZOWrPnbjzvpAhj0=/aCGZizTyvHItiOtanr4vs2pY-JWt7bTE9R2lB4l2B78=?refcode=5.22)******

I used my time on the (virtual) stage to discuss the need to pass Medicare for All, to enact a housing policy that provides a pathway to homeownership for black families, to end segregation in our school districts, to reform our immigration system, and to elect representatives who are willing to fight for their communities in moments of crisis. **[It's been a long time since we've had a champion like that in Congress, but with your support today, we can finally bring real leadership back to the heart of Brooklyn. Will you join me?](https://act.myngp.com/el/0SE2FnahfUAcJW-KeAy5qpG6aKDxZOWrPnbjzvpAhj0=/PSB_2LlEFykglQ8vrmIrj5mE4B_ujeC_GRgVTUr2tK0=)**

_If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: = Peter, In this new age of virtual campaigning, all aspects of our race have gone fully digital -- even the debate stage. So when News 12 Brooklyn hosted their first Zoom debate featuring myself and the other candidates, I used it as an opportunity to highlight the failed leadership that has led to this moment, and to share my vision for a way forward. = ****** I used my time on the (virtual) stage to discuss the need to pass Medicare for All, to enact a housing policy that provides a pathway to homeownership for black families, to end segregation in our school districts, to reform our immigration system, and to elect representatives who are willing to fight for their communities in moments of crisis. = If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: **** **** **** **** We have just over one month to go until Primary Day on June 23rd, and even less time until voters receive and return their mail-in ballots, so there's no time to waste: = Best, Adem **=** ****=** **=** **=** Copyright © 2020 Friends of Adem, All rights reserved. name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Phase Two is scheduled to start June 1st. Here's what will change.
body:
News about Rhode Island's COVID-19 response
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
For a few weeks now, we've been testing the waters of reopening. We are happy so far with the results that we're seeing, but we are being cautious not to go too fast. As of right now, our current set of restrictions is set to expire on June 1st. I know that these last three months have been difficult, but because of the hard work of thousands of Rhode Islanders, we are now looking at moving to Phase Two when the current restrictions lift. In Phase Two, we're going to be navigating our way forward. Our focus is on getting more Rhode Islanders back to work while continuing to prioritize public health and safety. Everything is still going to feel different - but most sectors of the economy will see some form of reopening, and we're going to get back to doing a lot of the things we've missed. Today, I want to talk about what life looks like in Phase Two.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PW0itZRtk2Yyh0IBHcEHQR1R_IQm1bFTtkEk1pbh6rsQ91-DHbv0HeLAe1PdfEXL8NyOLvJfJKt0YINA8sj28vnQMCOpIHGNFvvF_C23eG-4yAaVekOK0iecRM7-6XzQIm7rJWUFF7cfv-8fSgaYJy08nuEokkYU5zKuFLbPLyKfPQK973IMNjVuyGgle1nWz7vkhy-OY3y2jGhwHq-_9cRc-zsY0k1O&c=hHEX6iQTCoenDhrh7UAREQG84H7lgJScMjWA0Phg-g32xILgyqZFzA==&ch=lTPCoWyQ0aMzE1oG4fVwSliFjnwBNTlPrqRjW-ZUSK5VLGUi2yvC9Q==)
---
|
* Social Gatherings: I know how hard it's been to be away from our families and friends for months. Video calls are great, but they don't replace in-person interaction. In Phase Two, the social gathering limit will be 15 people. While that's a big change, the rules around it are still the same: you still need to practice social distancing, you still need to wear your face covering, and you shouldn't be seeing a different 15 people every day. I know that we're all anxious to reunite with your friends and family, but we need to take it slowly as we start to get together with the people we've missed.

* Travel: As we approach the summer months, how we'll handle travel is a question on everyone's mind. In Phase Two, the travel restrictions will largely lift - with some important distinctions. The 14-day quarantine will only be in place for those returning to Rhode Island from an area still under stay-at-home order or similar type of restriction.

* Restaurants: Our restaurant industry has been among the hardest hit over these past few months. We've worked hard to ensure restaurants can stay open for takeout, delivery, and outdoor dining. In Phase Two, we're going to allow indoor dining at up to 50% capacity. There will still be many other restrictions in place around cleaning, common areas, mask wearing and spacing out tables. Going out to dinner will still feel different, but we'll be finding ways to get back to business.

* Retail: In Phase Two, we'll be continue to relax capacity restrictions so more people will be allowed in a store at one time. We'll also be allowing malls to reopen - again, with restrictions. We can't allow large groups to congregate in open areas. That means you won't be able to gather in a food court or hang out in large groups in common spaces, but more of the retails stores we miss will be allowed to open.

* Offices: Rhode Island's businesses have done an incredible job adapting to the circumstances with teleworking, video meetings, conference calls and more. We still want everyone who can work from home to do so. In Phase Two, if it is necessary that some of your workforce be on-site, business can allow up to 1/3 of their workforce to return. Everyone is going to have to continue taking precautions around cleaning, screening, social distancing, and most importantly, staying home if you're sick and completing a coronavirus control plan template.

* Haircare and Personal Services: In Phase 2, hair services including barbershops, salons, and hair braiders will all reopen with capacity restrictions. In addition, lots of other personal close-contact services - nailcare, waxing, tanning, massage, tattoos - will reopen. The person performing the services will have to wear a mask, and the customer will have to wear a mask as long as practically possible. Customers should make appointments so we can avoid crowded waiting areas . Customers will have to space out, and cleaning will be very important.

* Gyms and Fitness Studios: Staying active and healthy is so important. In Phase Two, gyms and fitness studios will reopen with restrictions. Group fitness classes will also be allowed to resume. The name of the game is going to be maintaining your social distance, keeping your group consistent and planning ahead. We're asking fitness centers to go with a reservation system so they can prevent overcrowding and keep people socially distant. If you take a class, you should stay with the same consistent group.

* Outdoor Entertainment and Recreation: This virus thrives in crowded spaces, and so we still need to wait to reopen things like theaters and concert halls - places where maintaining social distancing is really challenging. However, we know it's a lot easier to keep people spaced out when we're outside and that the virus also doesn't spread the same way outside. In phase 2 we can start to allow outdoor activities to come back. This includes things like going to the zoo and outdoor activities such as mini golf and boat rentals, and visits to areas such as public gardens and the outdoor ground of historical sites.

---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PW0itZRtk2Yyh0IBHcEHQR1R_IQm1bFTtkEk1pbh6rsQ91-DHbv0HWKlEBOalriFdC8IRUe6t2iuUzntZpRSUP6tUAN3x-1ghEv-OLG6SSml_2lujceUj9tPLNACCtHnl7ZDMOaYN7ccVMmkIV5r_ier6nu0nJpa6waSXat1ptuFnee9vKnVDzhAAQakLynPDnizBeVk9EY=&c=hHEX6iQTCoenDhrh7UAREQG84H7lgJScMjWA0Phg-g32xILgyqZFzA==&ch=lTPCoWyQ0aMzE1oG4fVwSliFjnwBNTlPrqRjW-ZUSK5VLGUi2yvC9Q==)
---
| To stay up to date with Rhode Island's latest COVID-19 statistics, please check the Department of Health's [new data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PW0itZRtk2Yyh0IBHcEHQR1R_IQm1bFTtkEk1pbh6rsQ91-DHbv0HWKlEBOalriFdC8IRUe6t2iuUzntZpRSUP6tUAN3x-1ghEv-OLG6SSml_2lujceUj9tPLNACCtHnl7ZDMOaYN7ccVMmkIV5r_ier6nu0nJpa6waSXat1ptuFnee9vKnVDzhAAQakLynPDnizBeVk9EY=&c=hHEX6iQTCoenDhrh7UAREQG84H7lgJScMjWA0Phg-g32xILgyqZFzA==&ch=lTPCoWyQ0aMzE1oG4fVwSliFjnwBNTlPrqRjW-ZUSK5VLGUi2yvC9Q==) for all the information you need about the spread of coronavirus in Rhode Island.
---
| It's important that every reopening business - and every business that is continuing operations - completes a COVID-19 control plan. This plan will ensure that you've digested the public health guidance, that you've tailored the guidance to your business, that you're prepared to open. We have a template for this control plan on our [www.reopeningri.com](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PW0itZRtk2Yyh0IBHcEHQR1R_IQm1bFTtkEk1pbh6rsQ91-DHbv0HeLAe1PdfEXLWVG-crG8c2XQoYSd_0gpR-na5gkgYAiJV7f83VeE9TLOawmnNocQ0Sw8mu1pP6d51fl1nzA9-2KVOAuuwyjpXw==&c=hHEX6iQTCoenDhrh7UAREQG84H7lgJScMjWA0Phg-g32xILgyqZFzA==&ch=lTPCoWyQ0aMzE1oG4fVwSliFjnwBNTlPrqRjW-ZUSK5VLGUi2yvC9Q==) website.
There's a lot of change in Phase Two, and that's exciting. We have a lot to look forward to. But I want to be mindful that this virus is still very much with us. This Memorial Day weekend is NOT the start of Phase 2. If we go out and act like it is, that only jeopardizes our progress. I'm telling you now what's coming because our businesses need time to prepare and Rhode Islanders deserve to know what's coming down the pike. But we need everyone to do the right thing and hold on a little longer. We can't afford to go backwards
We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PW0itZRtk2Yyh0IBHcEHQR1R_IQm1bFTtkEk1pbh6rsQ91-DHbv0HeLAe1PdfEXLJf8HskPuI1US94ypPlkd4O2HadMpMWqYYZ1TOS4EZ8ogT_IX82UW2X75Q3hmcSJtJOG5zRILWjssAIGjBNyuwfwTTMuNVZnU-F8N--SI3vP96M0q18jR1w9GOhb5LYyTVKhha0waN8LXczjtXC4sWoO1Mab8IHRpTRVDcSQDeK_12o9VyWB8UshSKtcvd29caiYxH81AE13H5GrcKShwLQpRK-0S1HU0u17-lni2B7UYKwJW65xv39Gi_HKUdozeeEMTldo6qwk=&c=hHEX6iQTCoenDhrh7UAREQG84H7lgJScMjWA0Phg-g32xILgyqZFzA==&ch=lTPCoWyQ0aMzE1oG4fVwSliFjnwBNTlPrqRjW-ZUSK5VLGUi2yvC9Q==).
---

clean_body: News about Rhode Island's COVID-19 response | | | | | | | Hello friend, For a few weeks now, we've been testing the waters of reopening. We are happy so far with the results that we're seeing, but we are being cautious not to go too fast. As of right now, our current set of restrictions is set to expire on June 1st. I know that these last three months have been difficult, but because of the hard work of thousands of Rhode Islanders, we are now looking at moving to Phase Two when the current restrictions lift. In Phase Two, we're going to be navigating our way forward. Our focus is on getting more Rhode Islanders back to work while continuing to prioritize public health and safety. Everything is still going to feel different - but most sectors of the economy will see some form of reopening, and we're going to get back to doing a lot of the things we've missed. Today, I want to talk about what life looks like in Phase Two. | == | Social Gatherings: I know how hard it's been to be away from our families and friends for months. Video calls are great, but they don't replace in-person interaction. In Phase Two, the social gathering limit will be 15 people. While that's a big change, the rules around it are still the same: you still need to practice social distancing, you still need to wear your face covering, and you shouldn't be seeing a different 15 people every day. I know that we're all anxious to reunite with your friends and family, but we need to take it slowly as we start to get together with the people we've missed. Travel: As we approach the summer months, how we'll handle travel is a question on everyone's mind. In Phase Two, the travel restrictions will largely lift - with some important distinctions. The 14-day quarantine will only be in place for those returning to Rhode Island from an area still under stay-at-home order or similar type of restriction. Restaurants: Our restaurant industry has been among the hardest hit over these past few months. We've worked hard to ensure restaurants can stay open for takeout, delivery, and outdoor dining. In Phase Two, we're going to allow indoor dining at up to 50% capacity. There will still be many other restrictions in place around cleaning, common areas, mask wearing and spacing out tables. Going out to dinner will still feel different, but we'll be finding ways to get back to business. Retail: In Phase Two, we'll be continue to relax capacity restrictions so more people will be allowed in a store at one time. We'll also be allowing malls to reopen - again, with restrictions. We can't allow large groups to congregate in open areas. That means you won't be able to gather in a food court or hang out in large groups in common spaces, but more of the retails stores we miss will be allowed to open. Offices: Rhode Island's businesses have done an incredible job adapting to the circumstances with teleworking, video meetings, conference calls and more. We still want everyone who can work from home to do so. In Phase Two, if it is necessary that some of your workforce be on-site, business can allow up to 1/3 of their workforce to return. Everyone is going to have to continue taking precautions around cleaning, screening, social distancing, and most importantly, staying home if you're sick and completing a coronavirus control plan template. Haircare and Personal Services: In Phase 2, hair services including barbershops, salons, and hair braiders will all reopen with capacity restrictions. In addition, lots of other personal close-contact services - nailcare, waxing, tanning, massage, tattoos - will reopen. The person performing the services will have to wear a mask, and the customer will have to wear a mask as long as practically possible. Customers should make appointments so we can avoid crowded waiting areas . Customers will have to space out, and cleaning will be very important. Gyms and Fitness Studios: Staying active and healthy is so important. In Phase Two, gyms and fitness studios will reopen with restrictions. Group fitness classes will also be allowed to resume. The name of the game is going to be maintaining your social distance, keeping your group consistent and planning ahead. We're asking fitness centers to go with a reservation system so they can prevent overcrowding and keep people socially distant. If you take a class, you should stay with the same consistent group. Outdoor Entertainment and Recreation: This virus thrives in crowded spaces, and so we still need to wait to reopen things like theaters and concert halls - places where maintaining social distancing is really challenging. However, we know it's a lot easier to keep people spaced out when we're outside and that the virus also doesn't spread the same way outside. In phase 2 we can start to allow outdoor activities to come back. This includes things like going to the zoo and outdoor activities such as mini golf and boat rentals, and visits to areas such as public gardens and the outdoor ground of historical sites. | == | To stay up to date with Rhode Island's latest COVID-19 statistics, please check the Department of Health's == for all the information you need about the spread of coronavirus in Rhode Island. | It's important that every reopening business - and every business that is continuing operations - completes a COVID-19 control plan. This plan will ensure that you've digested the public health guidance, that you've tailored the guidance to your business, that you're prepared to open. We have a template for this control plan on our == website. There's a lot of change in Phase Two, and that's exciting. We have a lot to look forward to. But I want to be mindful that this virus is still very much with us. This Memorial Day weekend is NOT the start of Phase 2. If we go out and act like it is, that only jeopardizes our progress. I'm telling you now what's coming because our businesses need time to prepare and Rhode Islanders deserve to know what's coming down the pike. But we need everyone to do the right thing and hold on a little longer. We can't afford to go backwards We're all in this together. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use ==. | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov in collaboration with |

name: Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: The Great Re-opening has begun, Hundreds of Doctors Urge Re-opening, and Holding China Accountable
body:
**[Web version](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/m/1/49109489/02-b20143-8c16d8bf003c46569f9586b017608494/82/224/c15eb3b2-90d9-4a6e-ac25-e77c4009625e)** As of this week, all 50 states have begun the process of re-opening! This is fantastic news for Americans across our nation because the re-opening of our country means the re-starting of our great American economy – which will lead to better health and better prosperity. Over the past 9 weeks restrictive government lockdowns have cost us over 39 million American jobs. These are 39 million essential jobs that have been lost. These jobs were essential for allowing American citizens to provide for themselves and their families. The faster we can reopen America, the faster we can begin our economic recovery. | |
---
While a response to the unknown virus was warranted, we must also realize the unintended ramifications of continued government lockdowns. Earlier this week, with help from Tea Party Patriots, [650 American doctors signed a letter](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTQzLThjMTZkOGJmMDAzYzQ2NTY5Zjk1ODZiMDE3NjA4NDk0%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMA%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2NyaWJkLmNvbS9kb2N1bWVudC80NjIzMTkzNjIvQS1Eb2N0b3ItYS1EYXktTGV0dGVyLVNpZ25lZD9taWQ9NDIwNjAmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRI2Zyb21fZW1iZWQ&K=gZqVGpUsIMaY7Gn9Khjhiw) urging the President to end the “national shutdown,” calling the widespread state orders keeping businesses closed and kids home from school a “mass casualty incident” with “exponentially growing health consequences.”
[ READ THE WHOLE LETTER ](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTQzLThjMTZkOGJmMDAzYzQ2NTY5Zjk1ODZiMDE3NjA4NDk0%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2NyaWJkLmNvbS9kb2N1bWVudC80NjIzMTkzNjIvQS1Eb2N0b3ItYS1EYXktTGV0dGVyLVNpZ25lZD9taWQ9NDIwNjAmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFRI2Zyb21fZW1iZWQ&K=kUgdNiLu5C7OOORXtpiNlA)
Their voices need to be heard – it’s time for America to get a second opinion because the cure is looking worse than the disease.
|
---

[ ](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTQzLThjMTZkOGJmMDAzYzQ2NTY5Zjk1ODZiMDE3NjA4NDk0%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMg%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGVhcGFydHlwYXRyaW90c2FjdGlvbi5vcmcvbmV3cy9yaWRpY3Vsb3VzLW1lYXN1cmVzLz9taWQ9NDIwNjAmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=coZqrCSj9VC8ro0DrxIeTw)

**[Ridiculous Measures](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTQzLThjMTZkOGJmMDAzYzQ2NTY5Zjk1ODZiMDE3NjA4NDk0%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMw%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGVhcGFydHlwYXRyaW90c2FjdGlvbi5vcmcvbmV3cy9yaWRpY3Vsb3VzLW1lYXN1cmVzLz9taWQ9NDIwNjAmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=O9cpMW0K_PhrrIcCEmes6w)**
Many folks are feeling lockdown fatigue. Staying inside all the time, not socializing, with almost no end in sight. It’s a recipe for disaster. The physical and mental health of Americans is going to decline, not due to Coronavirus, but due to the lockdown itself. **[Read more...](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTQzLThjMTZkOGJmMDAzYzQ2NTY5Zjk1ODZiMDE3NjA4NDk0%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3ANA%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGVhcGFydHlwYXRyaW90c2FjdGlvbi5vcmcvbmV3cy9yaWRpY3Vsb3VzLW1lYXN1cmVzLz9taWQ9NDIwNjAmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=DYF1m1S2--sRHPWEJxOtpQ)**
|
---
As America continues to recover from the global pandemic caused entirely by Communist China’s deception and incompetence, the Trump Administration and the federal government are taking quick action to hold the Communist Chinese Party accountable.

From Sen. Ted Cruz introducing legislation labeling China as “the most dangerous geopolitical threat” currently facing the US to the Senate passing a bill to remove rogue Chinese firms from the US stock exchange, our government is working to hold China accountable – as they should.

China is the greatest threat facing America currently. We must work, not only to hold the Communist Party accountable, but to reduce our dependence on China. Vital American products should be made in America.

The Communist Chinese Party cannot be trusted.
|
---
Earlier this week, New York Governor Andrew Cuomo tried to blame President Trump for the more than 5,500 deaths in New York nursing homes.

Cuomo said, “I have refrained from politics... but anyone who wants to ask ‘why did the state do that with COVID patients and nursing homes,’ it’s because the state followed President Trump’s CDC guidance.”

This is categorically false, he is simply trying to pass the blame.

The truth is the Cuomo bears full responsibility. He was slow to respond to the threat of the virus AND he forced nursing homes to admit coronavirus positive patients. This action caused the infection to spread at an unprecedented rate.

Andrew Cuomo is to blame, not President Trump.
|
---

clean_body: As of this week, all 50 states have begun the process of re-opening! This is fantastic news for Americans across our nation because the re-opening of our country means the re-starting of our great American economy – which will lead to better health and better prosperity. Over the past 9 weeks restrictive government lockdowns have cost us over 39 million American jobs. These are 39 million essential jobs that have been lost. These jobs were essential for allowing American citizens to provide for themselves and their families. The faster we can reopen America, the faster we can begin our economic recovery. | | While a response to the unknown virus was warranted, we must also realize the unintended ramifications of continued government lockdowns. Earlier this week, with help from Tea Party Patriots, urging the President to end the “national shutdown,” calling the widespread state orders keeping businesses closed and kids home from school a “mass casualty incident” with “exponentially growing health consequences.” Their voices need to be heard – it’s time for America to get a second opinion because the cure is looking worse than the disease. | Many folks are feeling lockdown fatigue. Staying inside all the time, not socializing, with almost no end in sight. It’s a recipe for disaster. The physical and mental health of Americans is going to decline, not due to Coronavirus, but due to the lockdown itself. | As America continues to recover from the global pandemic caused entirely by Communist China’s deception and incompetence, the Trump Administration and the federal government are taking quick action to hold the Communist Chinese Party accountable. From Sen. Ted Cruz introducing legislation labeling China as “the most dangerous geopolitical threat” currently facing the US to the Senate passing a bill to remove rogue Chinese firms from the US stock exchange, our government is working to hold China accountable – as they should. China is the greatest threat facing America currently. We must work, not only to hold the Communist Party accountable, but to reduce our dependence on China. Vital American products should be made in America. The Communist Chinese Party cannot be trusted. | Earlier this week, New York Governor Andrew Cuomo tried to blame President Trump for the more than 5,500 deaths in New York nursing homes. Cuomo said, “I have refrained from politics... but anyone who wants to ask ‘why did the state do that with COVID patients and nursing homes,’ it’s because the state followed President Trump’s CDC guidance.” This is categorically false, he is simply trying to pass the blame. The truth is the Cuomo bears full responsibility. He was slow to respond to the threat of the virus AND he forced nursing homes to admit coronavirus positive patients. This action caused the infection to spread at an unprecedented rate. Andrew Cuomo is to blame, not President Trump. | Tea Party Patriots Action Honorary Chairman JennyBeth Martin — responding to President Trump’s executive order to waive unnecessary and job-killing regulations. | |

name: Genevieve Collins
subject: We've been busy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Collins for Texas, Inc
body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001latstumAJdjZUDf1XlS609XZssm5TyTFXPKmX5FxVisZk9SWJw76gkn_1u0fveqnkik6JX1T3I30_Hr7qNytntpNZ6bOiP04qUA0lN1w-qCrVmayAFT7z1umcY4J1RQfspj_QuSXg-cKkApE6t47Yk6Wnu62TRw4_1IJCnOZaaYWps2IGDrf7w==&c=8QtPeNnsqHve6-F3lT371Pr8K51W8w0GsbQNHD48QHFCZU7qx2w5Iw==&ch=4qhaDl4PRVrRk4DPrnK5ai6svujbRWfeY3x7mJ8soe0XBAo3s1n9QA==)

| Peter,
It has been an exciting week, and Texas is reopening! With Phase Two of Gov. Abbott's plan now underway, it feels as though some sense of "normal" is slowly returning to North Texas.
---
| It's not surprising that the reopening of Texas was our most commented on and shared social media post of the week. If you want to participate in the discussion, just click or tap on the photo on the right.
On Wednesday, we also had a great teletownhall with Senator John Cornyn. We had the opportunity to discuss the issues that are facing our district during these truly unique times.
We also released our second districtwide survey this week. This time, we asked you about how you are handling education during COVID-19, and how the various levels of government have been performing when it comes to education.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001latstumAJdjZUDf1XlS609XZssm5TyTFXPKmX5FxVisZk9SWJw76gnHT2tlFjJwOaEum4MI8IkWDwkwLxWRj34xU2qZiZkt86Bzai5IWd5Q_wBkKPIM1AxO_qYYuKjC4s9-E410W90UiitmnZ90zwKkQQ-rveufJFlBRhlwzxRe3-pN24qOq3TP0RKw_9deqvMSWKUej8bs=&c=8QtPeNnsqHve6-F3lT371Pr8K51W8w0GsbQNHD48QHFCZU7qx2w5Iw==&ch=4qhaDl4PRVrRk4DPrnK5ai6svujbRWfeY3x7mJ8soe0XBAo3s1n9QA==)

| In our weekly Small Business Spotlight segment, we highlighted SWEAT Gym. I spoke with Kegan and Katie about how COVID-19 has impacted their business, what new safety procedures they've implemented, and how they are approaching reopening now that gyms are able to welcome customers again. If you haven't watched my interview, [visit my Instagram page](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001latstumAJdjZUDf1XlS609XZssm5TyTFXPKmX5FxVisZk9SWJw76gnHT2tlFjJwOJjh6OF97xwj65kt2dy9n3xnq70Fwn1RjnKDE5tVRF-NP4RxkQh2c_xOnYjIruG7EQtaICTqYyl5MHp59ENA_BMqukwITDzy3ZjKMtIj_cUew6AsvfeTJ6Q==&c=8QtPeNnsqHve6-F3lT371Pr8K51W8w0GsbQNHD48QHFCZU7qx2w5Iw==&ch=4qhaDl4PRVrRk4DPrnK5ai6svujbRWfeY3x7mJ8soe0XBAo3s1n9QA==), or click/tap on the photo to the left to check it out now.
---
| As we head into the Memorial Day weekend, we think about those who have given their lives defending our nation. I am so happy that with the loosening of restrictions, I will be able to personally attend some Memorial Day commemorations this weekend to pay my respects to America's fallen.
I hope you and your family are staying safe and healthy, and that you are enjoying this slow return to normalcy.
Thank you and God bless,
---
|
---
| Genevieve Collins Candidate for Congressional District 32

name: =?utf-8?Q?Bobby=20Schilling?=
subject: The fight of Bobby's life
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bobbyforiowa.com/infobobbyforiowacom-2j2rkno6gz?e=90fad69796)
---
| |
---
| |
# _**The fight of my dad's life**_
Last night, I updated everyone on my dad’s successful cancer surgery. He’s going to fight this and win and our family is so grateful for your prayers and support during this time.

I also announced that in light of my dad not being able to campaign in the final days of the primary that we would be pulling our negative ads on our establishment opponent. I challenged her to do the same as a show of good faith.

_**She callously rejected my challenge within 30 minutes.**_

Here’s the thing: **our opponent is the establishment candidate. She’s been handpicked by the party and they’ve raised her a lot of money. My dad has been fought tooth and nail by the party and has built an incredible grassroots campaign. **

So much so that polling shows that he’s positioned to win this race.

No one wastes money on negative attack ads if they have a decent lead on their rival. That tells me her polling is showing the same as ours... he's eating her lunch.

Our opponent is in such deep trouble that she must continue running dishonest attack ads against a candidate who can’t even campaign in the final days.

_**She’s scared.**_

This is our chance to send a message to the party and the establishment and the hate-filled media. We aren’t going to tolerate a coronation and we aren’t going to tolerate your fake news.

Bobby is in the biggest fight of his life — and we need your help finishing this race!

In the final weeks we need to get our Get Out The Vote (GOTV) operation funded. We need to raise another $42,400 — and we can do it, but only with your help.

Will you consider making a contribution to our GOTV operation? Please click [here](https://bobbyforiowa.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f57658eeef5e9b1f9d6e8ab7e&id=ae83a1b477&e=90fad69796) to donate

Please forward this email to your family and friends to help spread the word and continue building this movement.

Let’s win this thing!

-Terry

name: Colin Lauderdale, Campaign Manager
subject: Campaign Announcement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Something incredible is happening here in Kentucky. The country is starting to pay more attention to this competitive primary and momentum is surging for our bold, progressive movement to defeat Mitch McConnell.
#
Something incredible is happening here in Kentucky.
After 16 Democratic State Representatives in Kentucky endorsed Charles, a newly released poll showed that a Democrat is polling neck and neck with Mitch McConnell. I can’t thank you enough for helping us make this campaign the unstoppable force it is today.
All the pieces are falling into place — **but if we want to continue growing this momentum, and close the gap between us and Amy McGrath by June 23 — _we really need to step up on our fundraising numbers too._**
So today we’re announcing our campaign’s biggest fundraising goal yet**: we’re setting out to raise $100,000 in the next 10 days leading up to our end of month deadline.**
**[I’ll be honest with you, this is a very ambitious goal and we can’t say for sure we’ll reach it. But if we do, we’ll have the resources we need to pull off a grassroots victory in June and then go on to defeat Mitch McConnell. clean_body: Something incredible is happening here in Kentucky. The country is starting to pay more attention to this competitive primary and momentum is surging for our bold, progressive movement to defeat Mitch McConnell. Something incredible is happening here in Kentucky. After 16 Democratic State Representatives in Kentucky endorsed Charles, a newly released poll showed that a Democrat is polling neck and neck with Mitch McConnell. I can’t thank you enough for helping us make this campaign the unstoppable force it is today. All the pieces are falling into place — but if we want to continue growing this momentum, and close the gap between us and Amy McGrath by June 23 — we really need to step up on our fundraising numbers too. So today we’re announcing our campaign’s biggest fundraising goal yet: we’re setting out to raise $100,000 in the next 10 days leading up to our end of month deadline. = = This is a really big moment for our campaign right now. = The country is starting to pay more attention to this competitive primary and momentum is surging for our bold, progressive movement to defeat Mitch McConnell. But I’m going to level with you — as Charles’ campaign manager, I know that having one good week isn’t enough to win. We need to continue growing this movement until the primary and invest more money than we ever did before on voter outreach and digital ads. This race is becoming increasingly competitive, and there’s no doubt that Amy McGrath’s team is planning ways to halt our momentum. = = Let's get to work, Colin **=** | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Booker for Kentucky

Friend, if we let Pelosi take control of this district it will not only expand her far left House Majority, but give her enough votes to implement policies like the Green New Deal and Medicare for All. Team Rodney needs your help! == Congressman Rodney Davis is a leader in Congress who has fought for this district since the very first day he took office. In Washington, he has worked to find innovative and longer term solutions to the problems we all face. Since the coronavirus pandemic began, Rodney diverted his attention to fully focus on making sure Americans had the resources they needed by working with local community leaders. == == == == == == Thank you, Alerts via Team Rodney Downstate Illinois is the #1 target for Democrats this year, can we count on you to rush in $15, $10, $5, or even $1, to help Rodney win this race? **== ==** Paid for and authorized by Rodney for Congress == PO BOX 344 Taylorville, IL 62568 This message reflects the opinions and representations of the Rodney for Congress. name: Elise Stefanik
subject: This was all preventable, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by LaHood for Congress |
body:
There’s no telling what the Communist Party of China will do next if they’re let off the hook. Chip in to help Darin and I hold China’s leaders | |
---
Friend,

My name is Elise Stefanik and I’ve been representing New York’s 21st Congressional District since 2015, the same year that Darin started representing your district.

_I know I normally don’t email you, but I’m really excited to work with Darin on something that affects my constituents in Upstate New York just as much as it affects voters like you in Central Illinois:_

_**Holding the Communist Party of China accountable for its handling of the COVID-19 outbreak.**_
[**CHIP IN TO HELP US HOLD CHINA ACCOUNTABLE**](http://links.lahoodforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d5887efe66684ada97976ef2e9d70137&_m=54e66f4173e74c029f1124ea9933b0d3&_e=8qshyMj-AmAY6YOH6xUPUJieevP8yg4dx-Y4rys5Q2u9ZKnX8u25MVTWPU6VhQNPKnwZJBMFwRlw2ItxhuuN07DznickyjZeDrA9vCFOHsrANJovhdegudNh0z2jEfP6CUPzG7WefA_2y0_VxnQubDl88ZKM-Z9-hCEzUPFTdJ_26hzKWsdEXWd-a2gzEGo4TWSs8Wi1SFZj3aI78PqrxINp1ZUlNiqH7-uRbZKp4fhAjltHlt1xqnzPbJ2IrioSc4SgWJieZUXxySv5q6Ouzs1rbP0IemJx5Y0qBqfuCE9mhoGPeMbzOS-jxxw4RzMb4R_4cqDwymMvxOi3sCmxqeR7qGsIyfmI_Px4Lx8FltZ8czXoJHBB_52wh3g032NLbvPDUPh902IY0dNcJrsyU0AhLVYQFLVKnA_pWYGqDfihmQTsF48TE2JPA0aIAkeMBWxVGuN24lHRrrvyx2mcRCWAh0HcQbyBxUAhZT1o2TE%3D)
Between business closures, canceled graduation celebrations, and the tragic loss of thousands of lives, there isn’t a single town in America that hasn’t been affected in some way by this pandemic.

**The worst part?_This was all preventable._**

If China’s leaders had focused on containing the virus – _**instead of arresting doctors and journalists who dared to share the truth**_ – we would be in a vastly different situation right now.
[**CHIP IN TO HELP US HOLD CHINA ACCOUNTABLE**](http://links.lahoodforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d5887efe66684ada97976ef2e9d70137&_m=54e66f4173e74c029f1124ea9933b0d3&_e=8qshyMj-AmAY6YOH6xUPUJieevP8yg4dx-Y4rys5Q2u9ZKnX8u25MVTWPU6VhQNPKnwZJBMFwRlw2ItxhuuN07DznickyjZeDrA9vCFOHsrANJovhdegudNh0z2jEfP6CUPzG7WefA_2y0_VxnQubDl88ZKM-Z9-hCEzUPFTdJ_26hzKWsdEXWd-a2gzEGo4TWSs8Wi1SFZj3aI78PqrxINp1ZUlNiqH7-uRbZKp4fhAjltHlt1xqnzPbJ2IrioSc4SgWJieZUXxySv5q6Ouzlg2Vz3d986iDzP3EiU4Qx8CmCpvULtUY43wYdPWm4TlzypCAET_PT8wy__SP8O80Y9e8mYaHVhdKEcgw5mkLjMyDCgzwihNv8PUkNGKOSolQj1daskroRmNfBJoT_RxutDJ5HbzJ_DyqHtVEjJCpdtFwLjWd1eAXawBY6Mh7VfyT_o68KYYXgxS2A4touCIMHVaswqdG601dnGshFMN-wc%3D)
Now, those same Chinese leaders have already broken ground on their next project: positioning China as the world’s political, military, and economic leader.

**We can’t afford to let China’s leaders off the hook for their actions which have cost hundreds of thousands of lives and trillions of dollars.**

That’s exactly why Darin and I are working together on the congressional China Task Force – to make sure that China’s leaders are held accountable for the damage they’ve done to the world.

_Will you help us make that happen, friend?_

[**Chip in any amount to help us hold the Communist Party of China accountable >>>**](http://links.lahoodforcongress.com/u/click?_t=d5887efe66684ada97976ef2e9d70137&_m=54e66f4173e74c029f1124ea9933b0d3&_e=8qshyMj-AmAY6YOH6xUPUJieevP8yg4dx-Y4rys5Q2u9ZKnX8u25MVTWPU6VhQNPKnwZJBMFwRlw2ItxhuuN07DznickyjZeDrA9vCFOHsrANJovhdegudNh0z2jEfP6CUPzG7WefA_2y0_VxnQubDl88ZKM-Z9-hCEzUPFTdJ_26hzKWsdEXWd-a2gzEGo4TWSs8Wi1SFZj3aI78PqrxINp1ZUlNiqH7-uRbZKp4fhAjltHlt1xqnzPbJ2IrioSc4SgWJieZUXxySv5q6OuzgeAakcsx6rCaRnd_8nMLFgRLcwuTPmqQ2hkKF_uBuViqGZSgXwJvlvOzDr0fL2Ip1VlTQMuB4r8_Tj9LhIDe-x9A18_R_yHOrz2DL7ngZMwl1nfPtOtidWR1KufWJVgk564a3pVgGddo6lRVaB5vZGugWVstXQalHJAGW7BCwpJMoeW3Nr8zkda14aeGXhMz-WEZ8VnAUv3kXY-51LYNdo%3D)

Thank you,
Elise Stefanik
Representative, NY-21

Election Day is 166 days away and Laura is on the verge of becoming the youngest member of Congress – beating AOC by two years.

Karen Giorno
Chief Strategist

name: Team Messner
subject: This Week: D.C. Shaheen Website, giving back to heroes, Pelosi's pork, and more
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
Tap or click to read more >>
Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative. [Contact Us](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/994bd6215bb0faa5c3d6b838967d376c/33153) | |
---
**Campaign Launches Petition Calling on Senator Shaheen to Reject House Stimulus Proposal and Website Aimed at Incumbent’s Lack of Leadership**
Corky Messner has announced the launch of a website targeting the progressive politics and leadership failures of incumbent Democrat Senator Jeanne Shaheen.
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/a2f98178ce616d34c3d6b838967d376c/33153)
“It is high time that our Senator remembers her constituents, and what we need from our leaders. Unfortunately, Jeanne Shaheen is a career politician who has failed to represent the values of most Granite Staters,” he says. “She has lost touch with her constituents as a member of a permanent and privileged political class whose elitist members stay in office for decades.”

The **[DCJeanneShaheen.com](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/b57d98adc3fd4e1cc3d6b838967d376c/33153)** website will regularly shine a much-needed spotlight on Senator Shaheen’s voting record, “as well as provide reality checks on how she spins that record and hides her true motives,” says Corky. “It will show that she cares more about keeping the Democrat leadership and her progressive cronies happy, ignoring New Hampshire voters and the issues that matter to them.”

The website this week includes a petition asking New Hampshire voters to demand that Shaheen oppose the $3 trillion stimulus package approved by the House under Speaker Nancy Pelosi and bound for the Senate.

The proposal, says Corky, “has less to do with helping New Hampshire families and businesses impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic and more to do with Democrats’ progressive agenda. Along with additional funding to help our economy, this $3 trillion boondoggle includes bail-outs, giveaways, and pork.”

The website petition asks voters to demand that Shaheen show leadership and abandon her practice of being in lock-step with Pelosi, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, and other Democrats in both houses.

It reads:
_Demand that Shaheen show leadership and abandon her practice of being in lock-step with Pelosi, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, and other Democrats in both chambers._
|
---
**Why is Senator Shaheen Not Commenting on Use of Small Business Stimulus Funds by Planned Parenthood?**
Corky is demanding incumbent New Hampshire Senator Jeanne Shaheen publicly address the alleged improper use of $80 million in stimulus funding by Planned Parenthood.
“The silence is deafening when it comes to an organization so closely connected to House and Senate Democrats,” says Corky. “Keeping progressive allies like Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer happy and not rightly calling Planned Parenthood out is apparently more important to our Senator than helping New Hampshire businesses stay afloat.”
Shaheen has repeatedly claimed she was a “lead negotiator” of the first COVID-19 economic stimulus package, specifically the CARES Act’s small business assistance programs, “yet she remains silent when it appears funds meant for small businesses have found their way into the coffers of a national organization with total net assets close to $2 billion and total revenue of about $1.7 billion.”

[**Read more on our website**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/89cd30e40920444cc3d6b838967d376c/33153).
|
---
**Corky Live on Facebook**
Corky was Live on Facebook this week, responding to messages and talking about the new website (see above).
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/624057fccfacc1dcc3d6b838967d376c/33153)
He also explained how Democrats position themselves as “morally superior” to conservative Republicans. An example close to home is Executive Councilor Deb Pignatelli, who during the Pledge of Allegiance at the start of a Council meeting said “...with liberty and justice for some.” Use **[this link](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/9c7c4e7da6997163c3d6b838967d376c/33153)** to learn the results of Corky’s research on the Pledge, and how conservatives aspire to achieve liberty and justice for ALL.
|
---
**Giving back to our heroes**
During National Police Week, to show his appreciation for our hard-working members of law enforcement in New Hampshire, Corky moonlighted as a pizza delivery man and brought a welcome meal to police officers in Wolfeboro.
|
---
**Who pays the bill?**
Instead of directing resources towards helping Americans, House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and other progressive Democrats last week passed a $3 trillion pork-laden spending bill under the guise of #COVID19 relief. It’s the most expensive bill ever passed – but it only supports Pelosi’s progressive agenda, and misleads Americans who need economic assistance during this pandemic.
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/f190cdde636a6a33c3d6b838967d376c/33153)

Check out our new website – **[www.DCJeanneShaheen.com](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/b5ea3d15cac68651c3d6b838967d376c/33153)** – and join the petition demanding that Senator Jeanne Shaheen reject this political ploy.
|
---
**Upcoming Events – Check Our Website!**
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/5eb5392e0d173e5bc3d6b838967d376c/33153)
We continue our Tele-Town Halls and other virtual events, so check our website regularly for information about the campaign and opportunities to ask Corky _your_ questions.
**_Coming up this week_**

_Tele-Town Hall (Dial-In at 603-506-4097): Rockingham County_ – Tuesday, May 26, 6:30-7:30 p.m.
_Facebook Live with Corky_ – Thursday, May 28, 6:30-7:30 p.m.
|
---
**Recent Poll Wins**

Momentum is building behind Corky's campaign to win the GOP primary. More and more Granite Staters agree he is the best candidate to flip New Hampshire Republican Red and defeat career politician Jeanne Shaheen in November!
|
---
**IMPORTANT CORONAVIRUS SUPPORT INFO**
**HEALTH INFORMATION**

New Hampshire Department of Health & Human Services Fact Sheet – **[Click here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/badcb829026c8d16/d86aa412988c13d6c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.

clean_body: Tap or click to read more >> Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative. | | Campaign Launches Petition Calling on Senator Shaheen to Reject House Stimulus Proposal and Website Aimed at Incumbent’s Lack of Leadership Corky Messner has announced the launch of a website targeting the progressive politics and leadership failures of incumbent Democrat Senator Jeanne Shaheen. “It is high time that our Senator remembers her constituents, and what we need from our leaders. Unfortunately, Jeanne Shaheen is a career politician who has failed to represent the values of most Granite Staters,” he says. “She has lost touch with her constituents as a member of a permanent and privileged political class whose elitist members stay in office for decades.” The website will regularly shine a much-needed spotlight on Senator Shaheen’s voting record, “as well as provide reality checks on how she spins that record and hides her true motives,” says Corky. “It will show that she cares more about keeping the Democrat leadership and her progressive cronies happy, ignoring New Hampshire voters and the issues that matter to them.” The website this week includes a petition asking New Hampshire voters to demand that Shaheen oppose the $3 trillion stimulus package approved by the House under Speaker Nancy Pelosi and bound for the Senate. The proposal, says Corky, “has less to do with helping New Hampshire families and businesses impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic and more to do with Democrats’ progressive agenda. Along with additional funding to help our economy, this $3 trillion boondoggle includes bail-outs, giveaways, and pork.” The website petition asks voters to demand that Shaheen show leadership and abandon her practice of being in lock-step with Pelosi, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, and other Democrats in both houses. It reads: Demand that Shaheen show leadership and abandon her practice of being in lock-step with Pelosi, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, and other Democrats in both chambers. | Why is Senator Shaheen Not Commenting on Use of Small Business Stimulus Funds by Planned Parenthood? Corky is demanding incumbent New Hampshire Senator Jeanne Shaheen publicly address the alleged improper use of $80 million in stimulus funding by Planned Parenthood. “The silence is deafening when it comes to an organization so closely connected to House and Senate Democrats,” says Corky. “Keeping progressive allies like Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer happy and not rightly calling Planned Parenthood out is apparently more important to our Senator than helping New Hampshire businesses stay afloat.” Shaheen has repeatedly claimed she was a “lead negotiator” of the first COVID-19 economic stimulus package, specifically the CARES Act’s small business assistance programs, “yet she remains silent when it appears funds meant for small businesses have found their way into the coffers of a national organization with total net assets close to $2 billion and total revenue of about $1.7 billion.” . | Corky Live on Facebook Corky was Live on Facebook this week, responding to messages and talking about the new website (see above). He also explained how Democrats position themselves as “morally superior” to conservative Republicans. An example close to home is Executive Councilor Deb Pignatelli, who during the Pledge of Allegiance at the start of a Council meeting said “...with liberty and justice for some.” Use to learn the results of Corky’s research on the Pledge, and how conservatives aspire to achieve liberty and justice for ALL. | Giving back to our heroes During National Police Week, to show his appreciation for our hard-working members of law enforcement in New Hampshire, Corky moonlighted as a pizza delivery man and brought a welcome meal to police officers in Wolfeboro. | Who pays the bill? Instead of directing resources towards helping Americans, House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and other progressive Democrats last week passed a $3 trillion pork-laden spending bill under the guise of #COVID19 relief. It’s the most expensive bill ever passed – but it only supports Pelosi’s progressive agenda, and misleads Americans who need economic assistance during this pandemic. Check out our new website – – and join the petition demanding that Senator Jeanne Shaheen reject this political ploy. | Upcoming Events – Check Our Website! We continue our Tele-Town Halls and other virtual events, so check our website regularly for information about the campaign and opportunities to ask Corky your questions. Coming up this week Tele-Town Hall (Dial-In at 603-506-4097): Rockingham County – Tuesday, May 26, 6:30-7:30 p.m. Facebook Live with Corky – Thursday, May 28, 6:30-7:30 p.m. | Recent Poll Wins Momentum is building behind Corky's campaign to win the GOP primary. More and more Granite Staters agree he is the best candidate to flip New Hampshire Republican Red and defeat career politician Jeanne Shaheen in November! | IMPORTANT CORONAVIRUS SUPPORT INFO HEALTH INFORMATION New Hampshire Department of Health & Human Services Fact Sheet – . New Hampshire Division of Public Health Services – . For general assistance, a 211 site to locate resources – . Centers for Disease Control: What to do if you are sick with coronavirus disease – . name: Warner Survey Team
subject: SURVEY re: Funding for essential workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Should states be able to use emergency funds to avoid laying off essential workers?
|
---
| | Should states be able to use emergency funds to avoid laying off essential workers?
[Take the survey >>](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d494/53d9897e/549f9cf9/1c887765/1577289937/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/s/warner-statebudget") Derek,

**Nearly a million government workers have already lost their jobs during the pandemic** , and thousands more are at risk because of restrictions put in place by the Trump administration. The recently passed CARES Act provides some funding directly to states, but it can only be used for a narrow set of COVID-19 related expenses. As states and communities across the country scramble to address budget shortfalls resulting from the coronavirus outbreak, they may be forced to lay off critical public servants -- including police, firefighters, and teachers.

Mark Warner is fighting to make sure that doesn't happen. He's pushing hard to give state and local governments the flexibility and economic relief they need to pay essential government workers during the pandemic. He recently spoke on the Senate floor in favor of increasing funding and loosening restrictions on existing funding, so state and local governments can use it to pay essential workers.

Do you agree with Mark that states should have access to the funding needed to pay their essential workers during this time? **[Please take our short survey and share your opinion.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d494/53d9897e/549f9cf9/1c887765/1577289937/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/s/warner-statebudget")**

Thank you,

The Warner Survey Team

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
In Michigan Visit, Trump Calls for Americans to Get Back to Work, Expects 'Epic' Comeback | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=76d6d6992f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**In Michigan Visit, Trump Calls for Americans to Get Back to Work, Expects 'Epic' Comeback**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f532b00842&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Detroit Free Press_
“Making his first visit to Michigan since the coronavirus pandemic began in the U.S., President Donald Trump on Thursday praised the ingenuity and pluck of Ford Motor Co. and its employees for turning its Ypsilanti components plant into a ventilator factory,” Todd Spangler reports. Ford and GE are jointly producing 50,000 ventilators.

🎬 **President Trump:** [Americans who want to work should be supported, not vilified](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=24e40747e2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Susan Rice’s Mysterious Email**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eda8f00bfc&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“The larger truth here is now undeniable: The Obama Administration spied on the political competition, it continued that spying even after Mr. Trump was elected, and then it tried to cover up what it had done,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes.
---
|
---
| [**Trump is Right to Hold WHO Accountable**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5cd2e6dd7a&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“President Trump's effort to hold the World Health Organization accountable for its bungled handling of the coronavirus is well justified. It is outrageous to expect taxpayers to continue supporting an international body that acts as a lap dog for Beijing,” the _Washington Examiner_ editorial board writes.

**🇺🇸****See it here:** [President Trump’s letter to WHO Director-General ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=456e60c370&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**It’s Okay to Acknowledge Good COVID-19 News**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c71496dc23&e=3ee4985eea)
-_National Review_
“The coronavirus has taken a heartbreaking toll on Americans, but the course of the virus is not the same as it was a few months ago. We are on the other side of the curve. There are encouraging signs all over the country, and no early indications of a reopening debacle,” Rich Lowry writes. “The question now is whether the media and political system can absorb good news on the virus, which is often ignored or buried under misleading storylines.”

**MORE:** [“The Massive Trump Coronavirus Supply Effort that the Media Loves to Hate”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=51c3bfe3f1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Public-Private Partnerships Will Lead Recovery**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4a69292033&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Detroit News_
“It took nearly a decade for Michigan’s small businesses to fully recover from the Great Recession of 2008-09. I’m optimistic these vital businesses will bounce back much quicker this time, thanks in large part to the indisputable success of the Paycheck Protection Program,” Small Business Administrator Jovita Carranza writes.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f578d5c92c&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c5528765e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=c0fef30d61)
---

body:
We're working every day to lay the groundwork for the general election, and we're just over halfway to the next FEC deadline. What happens between now and November is powerfully important -- and every | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28525722/45c30650/1cb2c9509/66e92be8/1514113088/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
